title: Add per-market funding-rate cap
summary: Read the keeper entry point first, then the helper it now delegates clamping to, then where the cap is stored, and finally the test. The cap is the whole change; everything else supports it.

steps.title: Entry point: settleFunding() now clamps the rate
steps.why: Start here — this is the function the keeper calls each epoch. The new clamp on line 132 is the core behavior; everything else exists to support it. Verify the clamp happens before the rate is applied, not after.

steps.title: The helper it delegates to: _capRate() (new)
steps.why: settleFunding() hands the clamping off here. The unchecked block on 50 is safe because cap is guaranteed <= type(uint128).max by the require on line 44 — confirm that invariant holds.

steps.title: Where the cap is read from: marketConfig
steps.why: Unchanged, but you need to see it: _capRate() reads fundingRateCap from this struct. It's per-market, so each market can set its own ceiling. Nothing here changed — just orienting you.

steps.title: Test: cap is enforced at the boundary
steps.why: Confirms the cap clamps an over-limit rate and leaves an under-limit rate untouched. Check that the boundary case (rate == cap) is asserted, since that's the off-by-one risk in _capRate().
